raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.5,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.5,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The abstract makes specific and potentially inconsistent technical claims—e.g., asserting closed-form Beta-Bernoulli posterior for z while simultaneously invoking SVI with a mean-field guide for theta—that must be checked for internal logical coherence; a technical 'check' makes this load-bearing.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative claims (38% rank-inversion reduction relative to Dubois et al., Spearman rho=0.91) constitute checkable evidence-claim links; verifying their grounding is central under a scientific-review interpretation.","R1.4":"BiasFlow's novelty claim rests on the non-parametric prior over latent preferences contrasted with linear-Gaussian baselines; checking this delta against the cited prior work is within reasonable scope of 'check this'.","R1.6":"'Check this' could plausibly be a pure proofreading/writing-polish request, which would make R1.6 load-bearing; until clarified it is assigned a central-but-not-load-bearing weight."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I perform a technical/scientific review (checking internal consistency of the probabilistic model and inference procedure, and scrutinising the quantitative performance claims) or a writing/proofreading review (checking prose clarity, grammar, and narrative flow)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The abstract makes specific and potentially inconsistent technical claims—e.g., asserting closed-form Beta-Bernoulli posterior for z while simultaneously invoking SVI with a mean-field guide for theta—that must be checked for internal logical coherence; a technical 'check' makes this load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims (38% rank-inversion reduction relative to Dubois et al., Spearman rho=0.91) constitute checkable evidence-claim links; verifying their grounding is central under a scientific-review interpretation.
rationale.R1.4: BiasFlow's novelty claim rests on the non-parametric prior over latent preferences contrasted with linear-Gaussian baselines; checking this delta against the cited prior work is within reasonable scope of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.6: 'Check this' could plausibly be a pure proofreading/writing-polish request, which would make R1.6 load-bearing; until clarified it is assigned a central-but-not-load-bearing weight.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I perform a technical/scientific review (checking internal consistency of the probabilistic model and inference procedure, and scrutinising the quantitative performance claims) or a writing/proofreading review (checking prose clarity, grammar, and narrative flow)?
